navSections.title: Foundations
navSections.blurb: HTTP vocabulary: resources, methods, and status codes that every API consumer relies on.

navSections.title: API Structure
navSections.blurb: Resource modeling, URLs, payloads, and list semantics — how clients navigate your surface area.

navSections.title: Gateways & real-time clients
navSections.blurb: Ingress, connection upgrades, and service-to-service callbacks — the HTTP/WebSocket layer, not message-broker topology (see System Design → messaging).

items.label: API Gateway (routing, TLS termination, auth at the edge)
Event-driven APIs and callbacks between services
Error handling in event-driven and async APIs

navSections.title: Performance & Scalability
navSections.blurb: Protecting backends and shaping traffic at the HTTP boundary (429s, quotas, cache headers). In-process token buckets, semaphores, and worker-pool throttling live under Concurrency.

navSections.title: Reliability
navSections.blurb: How clients and gateways behave when the network or dependencies misbehave.

navSections.title: Security
navSections.blurb: Identity, authorization, and validating untrusted input at the API edge — XSS, SQL injection, crypto, and secrets depth live under Security.

navSections.title: Advanced
navSections.blurb: Versioning, alternate RPC styles, and outbound hooks — still the contract layer, not broker internals.

concepts.title: Rate Limiting
concepts.summary: Protect your API with token-bucket or sliding-window limits: return 429 when clients exceed quotas instead of melting your service.

concepts.title: Retries & Backoff
concepts.summary: Exponential backoff and jitter for client retries — avoid retry storms while still recovering from transient failures.

concepts.title: Circuit Breaker
concepts.summary: Stop calling a failing downstream from every request — open the circuit, fail fast, and half-open to probe recovery.
